result:
All fixes are built, verified, and the local dev server has been shut down. Here's what was wrong and what changed:

**Root cause:** Lumina pivoted from an old product ("InboxZap," an email-cleanup tool) to an AI meeting assistant, but the technical migration was never finished — the site was leaking the old brand and had several genuinely broken routes.

**Fixed:**
1. **Brand mismatch** — `layout.tsx` metadata still said "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes"; now correctly reflects Lumina everywhere, plus added `metadataBase`, Organization/SoftwareApplication JSON-LD, and a real dynamically-generated OG image (the old one pointed at a PNG that didn't exist in `public/`).
2. **404s on nav-promised pages** — `/features` and `/pricing` were linked in the header but had no route files at all (confirmed via a clean `next build`). Built both out.
3. **Broken FAQ redirect** — `/faq` permanently redirected to `/#faq`, but no `#faq` anchor existed anywhere. Added a real FAQ section with 5 Q&As and `FAQPage` schema (also a low-effort shot at featured snippets).
4. **Blog was fundamentally broken** — the index page was a client component fetching `/api/posts`, which doesn't exist, so it would spin forever with zero content ever reaching Googlebot. There was also no `/blog/[slug]` route at all, despite GSC showing 12 already-indexed post URLs pulling thousands of impressions. Rebuilt as fully server-rendered with per-post metadata, canonicals, and `BlogPosting` JSON-LD.
5. **Duplicate content cannibalization** — post-3 and post-7 were both "bulk delete emails" targeting the same query. 301'd post-7 into post-3.
6. **Content gap** — every single existing post targets the *old* email-cleanup audience; zero content targets Lumina's actual buyers. Added two cornerstone posts (AI meeting notes, meeting action items) and lightly cross-linked from the two most thematically adjacent legacy posts.
7. **Sitemap** — was missing `/features`, `/pricing`, and all blog posts (had a literal `TODO` stub). Now includes everything with proper `lastmod`.

Verified with a real `next build` + `next start`: all routes 200, redirects 308, sitemap lists all 17 URLs, OG image serves as real PNG, JSON-LD validates as proper JSON.
